question.text: Which of the following correctly describes the relationship between positions in the futures market?

answer.text: Total long positions always equal total short positions
Long positions are typically higher than short positions
Short positions are typically higher than long positions
There is no fixed relationship between long and short positions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Every futures trade has a buyer (long) and seller (short). Therefore, total longs ALWAYS equal total shorts. Open interest counts only one side because they're always equal. **The Trap:** Unlike the spot market where net buying pressure can exceed selling, futures are zero-sum — every long matches a short. **Cross-Exam Context:** This fundamental property is tested across all derivatives exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This zero-sum nature means every profit from a long position is matched by an equal loss from a short position (and vice versa).
